name: Starship Demo
first: First fully reusable launch system to attempt Mars landing
description: An uncrewed demonstration of the Starship Mars architecture: launch on Super Heavy, refill in LEO from a tanker variant, then trans-Mars injection of a fully-fuelled Starship and powered descent to the Martian surface. SpaceX's stated target is a 2026-2027 launch window; engineering reality (Starship still working through orbital flight reliability and on-orbit refilling has not been demonstrated) makes 2028 or later more likely. If it succeeds, it inverts the Mars payload economics — from thousands of kg to one hundred tonnes per landing.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Starship from Boca Chica or Cape Canaveral — first super-heavy interplanetary launch.

events.label: ORBITAL REFILLING
events.note: Tanker rendezvous and propellant transfer in LEO over multiple flights.

events.label: TMI BURN
events.note: Trans-Mars injection from fully-fuelled LEO state.

events.label: MARS LANDING
events.note: Powered descent to Mars surface — no parachutes, no airbags, no sky-crane.
